archetype: Swiss & High-Contrast (Archetype 4) - Adapted for CAD/B2B Construction SaaS

typography.heading_strategy: Chivo for all structural headings, module titles, and UI labels. Use uppercase and tracking-wider for overlines.
typography.body_strategy: Chivo for readable paragraphs. JetBrains Mono for ALL measurements, quantities, coordinates, tile dimensions, and data tables. Never use generic sans.

layout.strategy: The 'Control Room' Grid - Dense, functional, 1px border-driven. No soft shadows. Everything feels like a technical instrument.
layout.spacing: Dense and compact. p-2 to p-4 inside panels. 1px borders for all divisions.
layout.radius: rounded-none or max rounded-sm (2px) to maintain the CAD/Blueprint aesthetic.

takeoff_studio.left_rail: w-14 bg-slate-950 flex flex-col items-center py-4 space-y-4 border-r border-slate-800 z-20
takeoff_studio.right_panel: w-80 bg-white border-l border-slate-200 flex flex-col h-full z-20 overflow-y-auto
takeoff_studio.bottom_bar: h-8 bg-white border-t border-slate-200 flex items-center px-4 text-[10px] font-mono text-slate-500 justify-between z-20

layout.dashboard: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-px bg-slate-200 border border-slate-200, items have bg-white. Strict grid.
layout.landing_page: High-contrast sections. Hero with deep slate background, white text, and an orange CTA. Feature sections use the Swiss Grid strategy (asymmetric 12-col grids).

buttons.primary: bg-orange-600 hover:bg-orange-700 text-white font-bold rounded-sm px-4 py-2 text-sm transition-colors
buttons.secondary: bg-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-800 text-white font-bold rounded-sm px-4 py-2 text-sm transition-colors
buttons.outline: border border-slate-300 hover:border-slate-900 text-slate-900 font-bold rounded-sm px-4 py-2 text-sm transition-all
buttons.tool: w-10 h-10 flex items-center justify-center rounded-sm hover:bg-slate-800 text-slate-400 hover:text-white transition-colors

components.inputs: bg-slate-50 border border-slate-200 rounded-sm px-3 py-2 text-sm font-mono focus:outline-none focus:border-orange-600 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-orange-600 transition-all
components.tile_swatch: aspect-square border-2 border-slate-200 cursor-pointer transition-all hover:border-orange-600 relative group overflow-hidden rounded-sm
components.data_table: w-full text-left text-sm text-slate-600 border-collapse, th are font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-slate-500 py-2 border-b border-slate-200

interactions_and_motion.hover: Instant or very fast (100ms) transitions. Tooltips appear instantly. No floaty animations.
interactions_and_motion.focus: Sharp 2px outline using the orange accent color (#EA580C).
interactions_and_motion.active: Tools in the left rail get a solid orange background when active. Cursor changes to crosshair when a measurement tool is active.
interactions_and_motion.loader: Indeterminate progress bar (1px height) at the top of the canvas, or a spinning mono-styled loader.

imagery_and_assets.strategy: Technical, precise, and authentic. Real blueprints, clean architectural environments, high-res tile textures. No abstract illustrations.

image_urls.description: A blueprint of a building with a bunch of windows
An architect working on a draft with a pencil and ruler
a close up of a tiled floor with a red and white checkerboard pattern
Two construction workers review plans on a tablet

imagery_and_assets.icons: lucide-react (Standardized technical look with 1.5px stroke width).

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure all interactive elements and key informational elements have a data-testid attribute in kebab-case (e.g., data-testid='polygon-tool-button').
Install and use 'Chivo' and 'JetBrains Mono' from Google Fonts. Do NOT use Inter.
Implement the layout as a pure flex/grid HTML structure with strict 1px borders, avoiding Shadcn 'Card' components where pure dense divs work better for the Control Room archetype.
Use standard .js files for React components. Do not output .tsx files.
The takeoff studio canvas should have a dot-matrix background using Tailwind arbitrary values or inline CSS to resemble an empty blueprint/grid.

For the 3D visualization pane, use an absolute positioned overlay or a resizable split pane. If a library is needed, use Three.js/React Three Fiber, but ensure the UI overlay remains crisp and standard DOM.
Make heavy use of Shadcn's Accordion, Tabs, and Table components for the right properties panel, but customize them to be extremely dense (h-8 instead of h-10, text-sm instead of text-base).
NEVER use placeholders like via.placeholder.com. ONLY use the provided image URLs.
